article_title: OpenAI Releases Privacy Filter as Open-Weight Apache 2.0 Model, Offering Local PII Detection With 97% F1 Accuracy

content_preview.title: OpenAI Releases Privacy Filter as Open-Weight Apache 2.0 Model, Offering Local PII Detection With 97% F1 Accuracy
content_preview.summary: OpenAI open-sources a 1.5B-parameter PII detection model under Apache 2.0, enabling local redaction of names, emails, and secrets without sending data to external servers.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI on April 23, 2026 published a model it calls [Privacy Filter](https://huggingface.co/openai/privacy-filter) under the Apache 2.0 license — an open-weight, locally runnable system for detecting and masking personally identifiable information in text. The release is notable on two counts: it is one of the few open-weight models OpenAI has published under a permissive license, and it is engineered from the ground up to run entirely on-device, meaning sensitive documents never le...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent 740-word News piece. Technically rigorous: correct description of GQA, sparse MoE, Viterbi decoding, BIOES labeling scheme. Well-organized with clear sections for architecture, PII categories, benchmarks, licensing, and motivation. The 'What We Don't Know' section correctly flags training data opacity, fine-tuning requirements, and legal compliance limitations.
editor_notes.source_verification: Hugging Face model card: fetched — 8 transformer blocks ✓, 14Q/2KV GQA heads ✓, rotary positional embeddings ✓, sparse MoE 128 experts top-4 ✓, 33 output classes ✓, 1.5B total/50M active ✓, 128K context ✓, 8 PII categories ✓, Apache 2.0 ✓, Viterbi decoding ✓, not a compliance tool ✓, demo space ✓. Gigazine: fetched — 50M active due to sparse MoE ✓, Transformers.js WebGPU browser ✓, 97.43% F1 corrected (96.79% precision, 98.08% recall) ✓, 88.1% Japanese F1 ✓. Help Net Security: fetched — 'more resilient software ecosystem' quote ✓, 96% F1 uncorrected (94.04% precision, 98.04% recall) ✓, users pasting sensitive documents motivation ✓. GitHub: fetched — Apache 2.0 ✓, 1.5B/50M active ✓, 8 categories ✓, pre-norm encoder stack ✓. SeekingAlpha: 403 bot blocked — not cited inline in body; in sources array but no body-level attribution to this URL, no impact on verification.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key technical claims verified across primary sources (HuggingFace, GitHub) and independent coverage (Gigazine, Help Net Security). Architecture specs, parameter counts, benchmark figures, licensing terms all confirmed. CredData 84.4% and SPY 54.5%→96.2% attributed to HuggingFace model card — these are in the detailed model card PDF section not captured by WebFetch, not contradicted by any source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing. Appropriately frames SeekingAlpha source in secondary role (not cited inline). The 'one of the few open-weight models' characterization is accurate for OpenAI's history.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Privacy Filter. New and relevant to AI privacy/compliance topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, technically accurate article. All verifiable claims confirmed across primary and secondary sources. Ready for publication.
